calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar May-13-2026 Set For Case Management Conference On Nov-18-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Jan-16-2026 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Jeffrey M. Fisher Esq., / Kyle A. McLorg, Esq., Farella Braun + Martel LLP jfisher@fbm.com /
kmclorg@fbm.com Attorneys for IndustryVault, LLC for Plaintiff.
Erik M. Ideta, Esq., MACKANESS & IDETA, LLP (619) 359-8700 eideta@mackdeta.com Attorney for Defendant William H. Barnes

Hearing is held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WILLIAM BARNES' MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Defendant William Barnes' motion for judgment on the pleadings on both causes of action alleged in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff IndustryVault is denied as to the first cause of action for breach of contract and granted without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for breach of negotiable instrument. The first amended complaint sufficiently alleges that the statute of limitations for breach of the loan agreement was extended by the Consent document. The plaintiff's proffered construction of the Consent document is plausible and must be accepted as true on this motion. The second cause of action for breach of negotiable instrument fails because the loan agreement is not a "negotiable instrument" as that phrase is defined in Commercial Code 3104. This because the loan agreement explicitly states another "undertaking or instruction" which section 3104(a)(3) provides is inconsistent with a document being a negotiable instrument. Plaintiff has not shown, nor is the court aware of, any reasonable possibility that plaintiff could file an amended complaint to allge a viable claim for breach of negotiable instrument.

Proposed order submitted.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-08-2025 Set For Trial On May-18-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-28-2025 Continued To Oct-22-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
